questions.question: Handling an Angry Friend or Family Member
questions.display:
• I stay calm when someone is angry.
• I listen to them without stopping.
• I do not shout or fight back.
• I try to understand their feelings.
• If I made a mistake, I say sorry.
• I give them time to talk fully.
• I never blame them directly.
• I show care with kind words.
• I don’t take angry words personally.
• If needed, I give them space.
• Later, I talk again when they are calm.
• This way, the relationship becomes better.
• I keep eye contact to show I care.
• I tell them I value our bond.
• I try to end the talk with a smile.
